stats: Critical Strike increases the chance of dealing critical damage with your spells. It also interacts with numerous abilities for Frost. stats: Agility: This is your primary stat, found on all gear except trinkets and rings, and scales extremely well with increased item level. Agility increases your Attack Power and damage done with abilities. It also increases your armor while [spell=203720] is up, and dodge chance.
Stamina: This stat is found on every piece of gear except trinkets, and adds to your health. It is good as a baseline, but generally does not factor into consideration outside of item level.

Armor: This is also found on every piece of gear aside from rings and trinkets. It gives you physical mitigation up to a cap of 85% reduction. However, you cannot currently reach the armor cap. Every point of armor increases your effective health by the same amount, but gives less damage reduction.

Critical Strike: This increases your chance to critically hit, doubling both damage and healing when that occurs. Critical Strike rating is also converted to Parry rating, increasing your chance to avoid melee hits. There is a hard cap at 100%, but it is currently not achievable.

Haste: This stat increases the proc rate of nearly everything in the game, aside from traits and trinkets that provide stats on proc. It also reduces the cooldown of [spell=258920], as well as the recharge rate of [spell=263642] and [spell=203720], and reduces your global cooldown by up to a maximum of 100%, from 1.5 seconds down to 0.75 seconds. However, it continues to reduce other cooldowns beyond that, as well as increasing proc rates with no limit, so it does not get worse at any point.

Versatility: This stat provides a general increase to damage and healing done, as well as reducing damage taken at half the rate. It works against all forms of damage, so it is the most effective general purpose stat for damage reduction.

Mastery: This stat increases your Attack Power, as well as the amount of Agility converted to armor during [spell=203720]. Since half of the value applies at all times, and half is applied during [spell=203720], it is a fairly mediocre stat at this time, with [spell=203720] having such a low uptime, unless running [spell=218612], in which case it gains some value, but still not enough to outweigh the value of other stats.
Leech: This grants you a portion of your damage and healing, excluding the healing from [spell=219741], as healing. It cannot critically hit, and is not affected by Versatility.
Speed: This makes you run a bit faster.
Avoidance: This allows you to take reduced damage from AoE attacks. AoE attacks must be those which specifically deal damage to multiple players immediately, and not through a ticking debuff.
Indestructible: This causes your armor to have no durability, so it doesn't need to be repaired.

Most of Guardian\u2019s abilities can critically hit, with the exception of [spell=22842] and the extra healing produced from our Mastery ([spell=155783]).

Mastery: [spell=155783] increases maximum Health and healing received, as well as increasing Attack Power. This is a strong defensive stat, and due to the changes to ability damage scaling is also a decent offensive stat.

Versatility: Increases your damage and healing (including absorption effects), as well as reducing the damage you take. This is your strongest overall Secondary stat due to it benefitting every area of your kit. As a defensive stat it also becomes more valuable the more you have of it, due to the multiplicative nature of damage reduction effects.

stats: Agility is your Primary stat. Agility increases your Attack Power and damage done on abilities.

Weapon DPS increases the damage done by your weapon and abilities that scale off weapon damage, which will be most of your abilities. This stat will generally mean that increasing the item level of your weapon is highly impactful.

Critical Strike: Gives abilities a chance to strike for twice as much damage. In addition, Critical Strike also has special interactions with the [spell=392054], [spell=468701] and [spell=459550] talents. All of your pets inherit your Critical Strike chance.

Haste: Haste increases your passive Focus regeneration and reduces the cooldown of [spell=217200] and [spell=34026]. It also increases your auto-attack speed (which means more [spell=1264781] and [spell=468695] procs). Finally, Haste reduces the time of your global cooldown (GCD). This means that the more Haste you have, the smoother your rotation will be. All of your pets inherit your Haste for their attack speed.

Mastery: [spell=76657] is a simple damage increase to all of your pets (including talents that summon any kind of beast, like [spell=120679]). This also includes AoE damage that comes from your pets, such as [spell=199530] and [spell=115939]. It also increases the damage of your shots by half of that value.
Versatility: Increases all damage dealt and reduces all damage taken. This has increased value when using traits and trinkets that deal direct damage.

Your [spell=133] hits a target for 3000 damage. With 30% Mastery, this creates an Ignite that will deal 900 damage over 9 seconds.[ul][li]Ignite ticks every second, meaning that the target takes 100 damage per second. After 9 seconds, the ignite caused by the Fireball is gone.
4 seconds pass. The ignite now has 5 seconds remaining. Its "bank" is now at 500/900.
This creates an Ignite that would have dealt 1800 damage over 9 seconds, had it hit a new target.
Instead, the remaining bank of 500 damage is added to 1800 for a total of 2300 damage in the bank.
The new ignite ticks for 230 damage per second, 10 times over 10 seconds.
For every instance of damage that contributes to ignite, this process repeats.

stats: Strength gives parry chance against melee swings, increases the armor gain of [spell=53600], and attack power, which scales the power of abilities.
Armor reduces the damage you take from most physical non-bleed damage sources. Protection Paladin has several armor multipliers that make armor from gear more effective.
Stamina increases your raw health pool.
Block reduces the damage you take from attacks that you rolled a block event on. The only place to find this stat is the Shield slot.
Secondary stats - most items have two secondary stats, but there are exceptions.

Haste: This reduces the Global Cooldown of all abilities. It reduces the cooldown of our rotational abilities, thus increasing Holy Power generation. Increased Holy Power generation increases the amount of [spell=53600] or [spell=85673].

Mastery: Increases the effectiveness of [spell=76671] which increases your chance to block. You also have a chance to block periodic magic damage. Offensively, this stat also increases Attack Power, which scales the damage of class abilities. It does not work on external damage sources like trinkets or procs.

Versatility: This is the all-around stat. It increases damage done and healing done, as well as reduces all damage taken at half the value. Offensively, this scales all damage, including damage from items or procs.

Critical Strike: This increases both our damage and our chance to parry melee swings. The stat is good for DPS in all situations. But for survival, it usually shines when the damage profile is primarily melee swings, which happens in M+. For Single-target bosses or situations that are mostly magic damage, it is worse than Mastery defensively.

Protection Paladins also have an attack power to spell power conversion since a few spells like [spell=85673] work from spell power. Protection Paladins gain 1.01 spell power for every 1 attack power.

Block Chance: Which is your % chance to roll a block event on any incoming attack. For this to roll a block, the attack has to be considered blockable in spell data. This roll is triggered after the game checks if an attack is a hit, miss, parry, or dodge.

Block:  This is the damage you reduce when you roll the block event. The game will convert the value on your shield to a %, similar to what it does with armor, and reduce damage on successful blocks by that amount.
Armor and Block scales based on a constant K. Blizzard sets the K constant to tune content to the baseline item level they expect people to have when attempting that content.

stats: Strength gives parry chance against melee swings, increases the armor gain from [spell=71], and attack power, which scales the power of abilities.
Armor reduces the damage you take from most physical non-bleed damage sources. Protection Warrior has several armor multipliers that make armor from gear more effective.
Stamina increases your raw health pool.
Block reduces the damage you take from attacks that you blocked. The only place to find this stat is the Shield slot.
Secondary stats - most items have two secondary stats, but there are exceptions.

Haste reduces the global cooldown, which increases Rage generation, DPS, and damage reduction with [spell=190456]. It also increases the availability of [spell=2565]. It is the best secondary stat, hands-down.
Versatility is a plain increase in damage done and damage reduction. It increases Equipment damage, including Trinkets and special weapons! A good stat all around.

Critical Strike is a great DPS stat. In M+ specifically, it gets a lot of traction defensively because of the sheer amount of melee attacks being thrown at you, and oftentimes can parry dangerous tank busters or prevent certain debuff applications entirely. You should, however, not plan around this happening. As only a fool relies on chance.

Mastery increases both the chance to critically block and base block chance. This increases overall damage mitigation. The main shortcoming is that it doesn't directly improve your damage reduction against unblockable sources (such as spell damage or DoTs/Bleeds) -- although it does increase your Attack Power, which makes [spell=190456] stronger.
